cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Network Fundamentals
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Comprehensive introduction to computer networking concepts including network protocols, architectures, and technologies. Students learn fundamental networking principles essential for understanding network security and implementing secure network solutions.
learning_objectives: Understand network protocols and communication models
Configure basic network devices and services
Analyze network traffic and performance
Implement network troubleshooting methodologies
Apply networking concepts to security contexts

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Operating Systems Security
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Security principles and practices for various operating systems including Windows, Linux, and mobile platforms. Students learn to secure operating systems, implement access controls, and monitor system activities for security threats.
learning_objectives: Implement operating system security hardening techniques
Configure access controls and user authentication
Monitor system logs and security events
Apply patch management and update strategies
Understand malware protection and system integrity

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Network Security
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Advanced study of network security technologies and methodologies including firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and VPNs. Students learn to design and implement secure network architectures and defend against network-based attacks.
learning_objectives: Design secure network architectures and topologies
Configure firewalls and intrusion detection systems
Implement VPN and secure remote access solutions
Analyze network security threats and vulnerabilities
Deploy network monitoring and incident response systems

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Digital Forensics
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Comprehensive study of digital forensics principles, tools, and procedures for investigating cybercrime and security incidents. Students learn evidence collection, preservation, and analysis techniques while understanding legal and procedural requirements.
learning_objectives: Apply digital forensics methodologies and procedures
Use forensic tools for evidence collection and analysis
Preserve digital evidence and maintain chain of custody
Analyze various types of digital artifacts
Prepare forensic reports and expert testimony
covered_materials: Memory forensics and volatile data analysis
File system analysis and data recovery
Legal and ethical considerations in forensics
Forensic report writing and court testimony

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Incident Response and Management
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Development and implementation of incident response plans and procedures for cybersecurity events. Students learn to detect, analyze, contain, and recover from security incidents while coordinating response efforts and communicating with stakeholders.
learning_objectives: Develop comprehensive incident response plans
Implement incident detection and analysis procedures
Coordinate incident containment and eradication efforts
Manage incident recovery and lessons learned processes
Communicate effectively during security incidents
covered_materials: Malware analysis and reverse engineering basics

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Risk Assessment and Management
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Systematic approach to identifying, analyzing, and managing cybersecurity risks. Students learn risk assessment methodologies, threat modeling, and risk mitigation strategies while understanding business impact and compliance requirements.
learning_objectives: Conduct comprehensive cybersecurity risk assessments
Develop threat models and attack scenarios
Implement risk mitigation and control strategies
Communicate risk information to stakeholders
Monitor and update risk management programs

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Malware Analysis
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Technical analysis of malicious software including static and dynamic analysis techniques. Students learn to reverse engineer malware, understand attack mechanisms, and develop countermeasures while using specialized analysis tools and environments.
learning_objectives: Perform static and dynamic malware analysis
Use reverse engineering tools and techniques
Understand malware families and attack vectors
Develop malware signatures and detection rules
Create safe analysis environments and procedures

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Advanced Network Security
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Advanced topics in network security including advanced persistent threats, zero-trust architectures, and emerging security technologies. Students explore cutting-edge security solutions and develop expertise in complex network security scenarios.
learning_objectives: Implement zero-trust network architectures
Defend against advanced persistent threats (APTs)
Deploy advanced security monitoring and analytics
Understand software-defined networking security
Evaluate emerging network security technologies
covered_materials: Network behavior analysis and anomaly detection

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Cloud Security
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Security principles and practices for cloud computing environments including infrastructure, platform, and software-as-a-service models. Students learn to secure cloud deployments, manage cloud risks, and implement cloud security controls.
learning_objectives: Understand cloud security models and shared responsibility
Implement security controls for cloud services
Manage identity and access in cloud environments
Monitor and audit cloud security posture
Evaluate cloud service provider security offerings
covered_materials: Cloud computing models and security implications
Identity and access management in the cloud
Data protection and encryption in cloud storage

cybersecurity_missing_courses.title: Mobile and IoT Security
cybersecurity_missing_courses.description: Security challenges and solutions for mobile devices and Internet of Things (IoT) systems. Students learn to secure mobile applications, IoT devices, and connected systems while understanding unique threats in mobile and IoT environments.
learning_objectives: Implement mobile device security policies and controls
Secure mobile applications and development practices
Understand IoT security architecture and protocols
Assess and mitigate mobile and IoT threats
Deploy mobile device management and IoT security solutions
covered_materials: IoT device security and lifecycle management
BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) security policies
